name: Texmex Red Snapper
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds Red Snapper, cleaned, scaled, and head removed
1/3 cup Pimento-stuffed green olives, sliced thin
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a wide frying pan over medium heat; add onion and garlic and cook, stirring often, until onion is soft.
2. Stir in sugar, salt, cinnamon, cloves, and tomatoes. Cook, stirring, over high heat until a thick sauce forms (about 8 minutes).
3. Blend together lemon juice, water, and cornstarch; stir into tomato mixture. Cook until the mixture boils and turns clear; remove from heat.
4. Stir in chiles and capers. Rinse fish, pat dry. Place a 24-inch sheet of foil acrosswise in a large roasting pan. Grease foil lightly (spray with Pam).
5. Place fish on foil; pour hot tomato sauce over fish. Bake, uncovered, in a 400°F oven until fish flakes when prodded with a fork in the thickest part (about 45 minutes).
6. Baste frequently with sauce during the last 15 minutes of baking.
7. Skim watery juices off the sauce with a spoon; then stir the sauce to blend it. Lift foil, fish, and clinging sauce and slide onto a platter.
8. Drizzle remaining sauce from the pan over the fish. Garnish with olives and cilantro.
9. To serve, cut fish away from bones, then lift each serving.
